title: Bytes, Unicode, Newlines, Front Matter, Metadata, and Schemas · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs bytes, unicode, newlines, front matter, metadata, and schemas from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned decoded-document and metadata contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.
Choose normalization, identifier, comparison, sorting, and display policies while retaining original author text.
Recognize bounded delimiters, separate metadata bytes from body, reject malformed closure, and avoid executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.text: A review publishing team handles case 4631 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode UTF-8 content and validate bounded front matter without hiding data loss; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.

lines.label: assumption

Build deterministic Python evidence to decode UTF-8 content and validate bounded front matter without hiding data loss; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent dates, time zones, draft state, language tags, translations, authors, and stable IDs without locale ambiguity. Artifact under review: versioned decoded-document and metadata contract.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Time Language Identity, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Time Language Identity record says versioned decoded-document and metadata contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Time Language Identity.

For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Time Language Identity for versioned decoded-document and metadata contract.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Represent dates, time zones, draft state, language tags, translations, authors, and stable IDs without locale ambiguity.
